title: Where is blood scarcity in the EU?
subtitle: In 2023, around 16.5 million whole blood units were collected across the EU27, with Germany reporting the highest number at 3.7 million, followed by Italy, France and Spain. Among countries with larger populations, Bulgaria and Finland recorded some of the lowest figures, both below 0.2 million units.
textNote: This map represents the number of total donors (including first time donors) which donated whole blood or apheresis components for transfusion at least once a year. Meaning the same person could have donated more than once.
textSource: European Directorate for the Quality of Medicines & Healthcare
